purpose: Core orchestration library for ultrainit's 5-phase pipeline. Each shell module owns exactly one pipeline phase (gather, ask, research, synthesize, validate, merge) plus shared infrastructure for agent spawning (agent.sh), logging/JSON/cost helpers (utils.sh), and configuration/budget management (config.sh). Together they convert raw codebase analysis into Claude Code configuration artifacts.

architecture.overview: Flat module layout — one file per pipeline concern. Each phase module exposes a single top-level entry function (e.g. gather_evidence(), ask_developer(), synthesize()) that is called in sequence by ultrainit.sh. Modules source each other freely (agent.sh sources utils.sh; all phase modules source both). There is no dependency injection — shared state is passed via exported environment variables (WORK_DIR, AGENT_MODEL, AGENT_BUDGET, TOTAL_BUDGET, etc.). All intermediate state is persisted to .ultrainit/ inside the target project, making every phase idempotent and resumable.

subdirectories.purpose: Foundation layer: colored logging, phase completion tracking via state.json, JSON helpers, spinner for interactive terminals, and per-agent cost aggregation.
System setup: dependency verification (with platform-specific install hints), work directory creation, budget allocation arithmetic, and global variable defaults.

Agent execution engine: run_agent() for single-agent invocations, run_agents_parallel() for background-process parallelism, diagnose_phase_failure() for AI-powered error interpretation, and record_cost() for race-condition-safe cost tracking.
Phase 1: launches 8 core evidence agents in parallel (Stage 1), then dynamically spawns per-directory deep-dive agents based on structure-scout output (Stage 2, batches of 8).
Phase 2: interactive developer interview (5 questions). Handles piped stdin by redirecting from /dev/tty; degrades gracefully to NON_INTERACTIVE mode if /dev/tty is unavailable.
Phase 3: domain knowledge and MCP server discovery via WebSearch/WebFetch. Two agents run in parallel; entire phase skippable via --skip-research or --skip-mcp.

Phase 4: two sequential synthesis passes (docs then tooling) each consuming the 1M-context model. Includes context builders, token estimation, retry logic, and post-processing for angle-bracket sanitization.
Phase 5a: structural validation of CLAUDE.md, skills, hooks, subagents, and hook wiring. Auto-invokes a revision agent to fix failed artifacts without re-synthesizing from scratch.
Phase 5b: writes all artifacts to the target project. Backs up before writing, deep-merges settings.json and mcp.json, and never overwrites existing user-edited skills/hooks/agents.

architecture.data_flow: ultrainit.sh sets globals → sources all lib/*.sh → calls phase functions in order. Phase 1 (gather_evidence): 8 parallel claude -p calls write JSON to .ultrainit/findings/*.json. Phase 2 (ask_developer): terminal prompts write .ultrainit/developer-answers.json. Phase 3 (run_research): 2 parallel agents write domain-research.json and mcp-discovery.json. Phase 4 (synthesize): build_docs_context() assembles all findings into a single context file → run_synthesis_pass() pipes it to claude -p → writes output-docs.json; build_tooling_context() uses output-docs.json as source of truth → writes output-tooling.json; merge_synthesis_passes() jq-merges both into output.json. Phase 5a (validate_artifacts): reads output.json, runs validate-skill.sh and inline hook checks, patches failures via revision agent. Phase 5b (write_artifacts): reads output.json, backs up existing files, writes CLAUDE.md/skills/hooks/agents, deep-merges settings.json and mcp.json.

key_files.purpose: The execution engine the entire pipeline depends on. run_agent() handles resumability, budget gating, @file prompt syntax, stdin piping for large prompts, structured output extraction, cost recording, and error detection. run_agents_parallel() implements background-process parallelism via temp shell scripts that explicitly re-export parent state before re-sourcing libs.

The highest-cost, highest-value phase. Contains build_docs_context() and build_tooling_context() which carefully select/exclude fields from findings to manage 1M-context calls. Two-pass strategy and retry logic for transient API failures live here.

Phase 1 orchestration. Defines the 8 core agent invocations, critical-agent failure logic (aborts if identity or structure-scout fails, or if 3+ agents fail), and dynamic deep-dive batching based on structure-scout output.

Startup gating and budget arithmetic. compute_budgets() allocates phase percentages; set_agent_budget() divides per-agent; check_budget() gates every agent call. Platform detection and dependency verification with install hints live here.
Foundation for all logging, phase state (mark_phase_complete/is_phase_complete via state.json), cost aggregation across per-agent .cost files, and JSON helpers used everywhere.
Safe artifact writing: backup_existing(), deep-merge logic for settings.json and mcp.json, and the never-overwrite rule for existing skills/hooks/agents. Critical for idempotent re-runs.
Quality enforcement: generic-phrase detection, prohibition-alternative pairing checks, hook shebang/pipefail/stdin validation, and the revision agent that patches only failed artifacts.
Phase 3 web research. Shows the pattern for writing prompts to files before passing them to run_agent() — necessary when prompts contain apostrophes, parentheses, or backticks.
Phase 2 interactive input with /dev/tty fallback for curl-pipe-bash installations. Shows how to make Enter-to-accept proposed answers work with fd 3 redirection.

patterns.name: Resumable phase tracking
patterns.description: Every phase entry function calls is_phase_complete(phase) first and returns 0 immediately if already done (unless FORCE=true). is_phase_complete reads a timestamp from $WORK_DIR/state.json written by mark_phase_complete(). Individual agents also skip if their findings file already exists. This means partial failures can be retried by simply re-running — only incomplete agents execute.

patterns.name: Parallel agent execution via temp scripts
patterns.description: run_agents_parallel() receives agent invocation strings, writes each to a temp bash script under mktemp -d, and spawns them with bash script &. Each script explicitly exports parent globals (WORK_DIR, AGENT_BUDGET, etc.) before re-sourcing lib files — this prevents re-sourcing config.sh from overwriting already-computed values. PIDs are collected in an array; wait loops count failures. Temp dir is rm -rf'd at end.

patterns.name: Per-agent cost files (parallel-safe budget tracking)
patterns.description: Each agent call records its cost to $WORK_DIR/costs/${agent}.cost in format 'phase|agent|cost'. No locking needed because each agent has a unique file. print_cost_summary() and check_budget() aggregate by globbing all *.cost files and summing with awk/bc. Budget is checked before every agent invocation; if cumulative spend >= TOTAL_BUDGET, the agent is skipped and a warning is logged.

patterns.name: @file prompt syntax and stdin piping
patterns.description: When prompts contain shell-special characters (apostrophes, parentheses, backticks), they are written to a temp file and passed as @filepath to run_agent(). run_agent detects the @ prefix and reads the file content before calling claude. For prompts >100KB (synthesis context), content is piped via stdin using 'cat context | claude -p -' to avoid ARG_MAX limits. Both patterns appear in gather.sh (module prompts) and research.sh.

patterns.name: Two-pass synthesis with context partitioning
patterns.description: Pass 1 (docs) feeds all raw findings to produce CLAUDE.md. Pass 2 (tooling) uses the Pass 1 CLAUDE.md as source of truth rather than raw findings — it gets the distilled architecture doc plus only tool-relevant findings (tooling, security, commands, patterns, MCP). This reduces Pass 2 context size while keeping it consistent with Pass 1. build_tooling_context() deliberately excludes key_files details, domain_terms, and skill_opportunities from module analyses.

patterns.name: Critical-agent failure gating
patterns.description: After Stage 1 of gather, get_failed_agents() checks which agents are missing findings files. If identity or structure-scout are missing, or if 3+ agents failed, the pipeline aborts. diagnose_phase_failure() collects the last 50 lines of each failed agent's stderr and calls a Haiku agent to root-cause and suggest fixes. Non-critical failures (1-2 agents) log a warning but allow the pipeline to continue with partial data.

patterns.name: Safe artifact merge (never overwrite user files)
patterns.description: write_artifacts() checks for file existence before writing each skill, hook, and subagent. If the file already exists it is skipped with a log message. backup_existing() copies all files that will be overwritten (CLAUDE.md, settings.json) to a timestamped backup dir before any writes. merge_settings() and write_mcp_config() deep-merge into existing files rather than replacing them — hooks are appended per event type; MCP servers are added without removing existing ones.

conventions.naming: Phase entry functions match their file: gather_evidence() in gather.sh, ask_developer() in ask.sh, run_research() in research.sh, synthesize() in synthesize.sh, validate_artifacts() in validate.sh, write_artifacts() in merge.sh. Exported globals are SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE (WORK_DIR, AGENT_MODEL, TOTAL_BUDGET). Local variables are snake_case. Agent names match their findings file: agent 'git-forensics' writes findings/git-forensics.json.
conventions.imports: Each lib file sources its dependencies at call time via source or . — no explicit import ordering enforced. ultrainit.sh sources all lib files before calling any phase. Agent subshells re-source utils.sh, config.sh, and agent.sh in that order (utils first, config second since it depends on utils logging).
conventions.error_handling: set -euo pipefail in all scripts. Errors from individual agents are non-fatal by default — run_agent returns 1 on failure, caller decides to abort or continue. Phase-level decisions: gather aborts on critical agent failure; research/ask mark non-fatal. jq calls use 2>/dev/null || echo '' to return empty string on invalid JSON rather than exiting. All agent stderr goes to $WORK_DIR/logs/${name}.stderr, not the terminal (unless --verbose).
other: Budget arithmetic uses bc for floating-point safety (not bash integer arithmetic); bc -l used for division
JSON validation before writes: jq empty < output_file || abort pattern prevents corrupt findings from propagating
Temp dirs always created with mktemp -d and cleaned up with rm -rf in all code paths
Phase completion written atomically: jq injects timestamp, then overwrites state.json only if jq succeeds
Module safe-name generation: sed strips /, ., spaces, parens; leading/trailing hyphens removed; falls back to 'root' if result is empty
Spinner (spin()) only runs when stdout is a terminal: checked with [[ -t 1 ]]
Cost files use pipe-separated format (phase|agent|cost) parsed with awk, not JSON, to avoid jq overhead in tight budget-check loops

internal: schemas/ — JSON schemas consumed by run_agent() via --json-schema; each agent has a corresponding schema file
prompts/ — System prompt .md files auto-loaded by run_agent() if $SCRIPT_DIR/prompts/${name}.md exists
scripts/validate-skill.sh — Called by validate.sh for each generated skill
scripts/validate-subagent.sh — Called by validate.sh for each generated subagent
ultrainit.sh — Sources all lib files and calls phase entry functions in order

external_key.name: claude CLI
external_key.purpose: All agent invocations: claude -p with --output-format json, --json-schema, --allowedTools, --max-budget-usd. Also claude auth status for startup validation.

external_key.name: jq
external_key.purpose: All JSON parsing, extraction, merging (jq -s '.[0] * .[1]' for deep merge), and validation (jq empty). Used in every lib file.

external_key.name: bc
external_key.purpose: Floating-point budget arithmetic: division and comparison. Bash arithmetic only handles integers; bc -l used for phase/agent budget allocation.

external_key.name: git
external_key.purpose: git-forensics agent (Bash git:* tool permission). Also git rev-parse --show-toplevel used in config.sh to find repo root.

external_key.name: mktemp
external_key.purpose: Safe temp file and directory creation for parallel agent scripts and synthesis context files.

external_key.name: sed / awk / grep
external_key.purpose: Text processing in validation (generic phrase detection), cost file parsing, module safe-name generation, and angle-bracket sanitization in postprocess_descriptions().

domain_terms.meaning: Structured JSON output written by each agent to .ultrainit/findings/${name}.json. The canonical intermediate representation between phases.
A single large-context claude -p invocation that reads all findings and produces a complete artifact set. Two passes run sequentially: docs (Pass 1) then tooling (Pass 2).
The gather agent that maps the full directory tree with role/priority classification. Its output drives which directories get deep-dive module-analyzer agents. Critical — pipeline aborts if it fails.
module-analyzer agent instances spawned one per high/medium-priority directory identified by structure-scout. Run in batches of 8 after the 8 core Stage 1 agents.
The model used for synthesis passes (default: sonnet[1m] — the 1M context window variant). Separate from AGENT_MODEL used for gather/research agents.
Per-agent USD cap passed to claude as --max-budget-usd. Computed as phase_budget / agent_count. Prevents any single agent from consuming the entire budget.
Fraction of TOTAL_BUDGET allocated to a phase: 50% gather, 10% research, 30% synthesis, 10% validation.
A claude -p call inside validate.sh that receives failed artifact content and validation issues, and returns fixed versions. Patches only failed items without re-running synthesis.

The outer JSON wrapper claude -p --output-format json returns: { is_error, errors, structured_output, result, total_cost_usd }. run_agent() extracts .structured_output (with schema) or .result (without).
A directory path sanitized for use as an agent name and filename: slashes, dots, spaces, parens stripped; leading/trailing hyphens removed. e.g. 'src/api/routes' → 'src-api-routes'.

gotchas.issue: Re-sourcing config.sh in subshells can overwrite computed parent values
gotchas.solution: run_agents_parallel() explicitly writes parent globals as literal export statements into each temp script before sourcing libs. The exported values win because they are set before source runs. Never add logic to config.sh that overwrites a variable if it is already exported.

gotchas.issue: Prompts with shell-special characters (apostrophes, parens, backticks) break inline agent calls
gotchas.solution: Write the prompt to a file under $WORK_DIR/prompts/ and pass @filepath as the prompt argument to run_agent(). The @ prefix triggers file-read behavior. This is mandatory for domain-research, mcp-discovery, and module-analyzer prompts.

gotchas.issue: structure-scout failure silently degrades deep-dive quality
gotchas.solution: If structure-scout fails, run_fallback_module_analyzers() uses a crude top-level directory scan. The fallback misses nested modules and skips priority classification. Treat structure-scout as a critical dependency and investigate its stderr log (logs/structure-scout.stderr) before assuming deep-dive results are complete.

gotchas.issue: Budget is cumulative across retries — retrying a failed phase consumes additional budget
gotchas.solution: check_budget() sums all .cost files including previous runs. If a synthesis pass fails after 3 retries, the accumulated cost from those attempts counts against TOTAL_BUDGET. Use --budget to set a higher limit when debugging, or remove specific .cost files manually to reset individual agent budgets.

gotchas.issue: Existing skills/hooks/agents are silently skipped on re-run
gotchas.solution: write_artifacts() skips any file that already exists in .claude/. Use --overwrite to remove existing config before analysis (backs up first). Without --overwrite, ultrainit.sh will not update stale skills if the underlying synthesis improved.

gotchas.issue: Synthesis context can exceed model limits even with build_*_context() trimming
gotchas.solution: estimate_tokens() uses a rough bytes/4 heuristic. Actual token count may be higher for dense JSON. If synthesis fails with a context-length error in logs/synthesis-docs.stderr, reduce scope with --skip-research or selectively delete low-priority module findings from .ultrainit/findings/ before rerunning.

gotchas.issue: Angle brackets in LLM-generated descriptions break YAML frontmatter parsing
gotchas.solution: postprocess_descriptions() in synthesize.sh replaces < and > with double-quotes in skill/agent descriptions. This is a heuristic workaround. If a skill description still contains raw angle brackets after synthesis, validate-skill.sh will catch it and the revision agent will attempt to fix it.

gotchas.issue: ask.sh reads from /dev/tty, not stdin, when piped — but /dev/tty may not exist in containers
gotchas.solution: ask_developer() checks if /dev/tty is available. If not, it falls back to NON_INTERACTIVE mode. Always pass --non-interactive for CI/Docker environments rather than relying on the auto-detection.

commands.purpose: Run bats unit tests for lib/ functions inside Docker (no real Claude calls)
Run the full bats test suite (unit + integration + edge + scripts)
Re-run all phases with agent stderr visible in terminal
Run analysis but do not write any files to the target project
Remove existing .claude/ config (with backup), then run fresh generation
Inspect synthesis Pass 1 errors when synthesis fails
Check which phases have completed (for resumability debugging)
Manually sum total spend across all agent cost files

skill_opportunities.name: add-gather-agent
skill_opportunities.description: Scaffold a new Phase 1 gather agent: create the prompt file in prompts/, schema in schemas/, wire it into gather.sh's run_agents_parallel() call, and add a fixture to tests/fixtures/findings/.
workflow_steps: Determine agent name, purpose, model tier (haiku vs sonnet), and required tools
Add run_agent invocation string to the run_agents_parallel call in gather_evidence()
Decide if it is a critical agent (add to failure-gating list if so)
Add agent to get_failed_agents() check list

skill_opportunities.name: debug-agent-failure
skill_opportunities.description: Triage a failed agent run: read its stderr log, check the findings file, inspect budget spend, and run diagnose_phase_failure() manually to get an AI diagnosis.
workflow_steps: Identify failed agent name from terminal output
Check if .ultrainit/findings/<name>.json exists and is valid JSON
Sum .ultrainit/costs/<name>.cost to check if budget was exhausted
Re-run with --force --verbose to get fresh stderr output in terminal
If API error, check claude auth status and retry

skill_opportunities.name: tune-synthesis-context
skill_opportunities.description: Reduce or expand what build_docs_context() and build_tooling_context() include when synthesis hits context-length errors or produces low-quality output.
workflow_steps: Check estimate_tokens() output in logs for both passes
Identify which finding files are largest (du -sh .ultrainit/findings/*.json)
Edit build_docs_context() or build_tooling_context() in lib/synthesize.sh to add/remove fields
Delete .ultrainit/synthesis/output-docs.json and/or output-tooling.json to force re-synthesis
Re-run with --force to test new context size
